Class ProgramVariable

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.logic.op.AbstractOperator
org.key_project.logic.op.AbstractSortedOperator
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.JAbstractSortedOperator
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.ProgramVariable
All Implemented Interfaces:
Expression, ModelElement, ProgramElement, ReferencePrefix, ReferenceSuffix, SourceElement, TerminalProgramElement, EqualsModProperty<SourceElement>, IProgramVariable, ProgramInLogic, Sorted, Named, Operator, ParsableVariable, SortedOperator, Sorted, SyntaxElement, TerminalSyntaxElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
LocationVariable, ProgramConstant
public abstract class ProgramVariable extends JAbstractSortedOperator implements Expression, ReferencePrefix, IProgramVariable, ParsableVariable, ReferenceSuffix, ProgramInLogic
The objects of this class represent program variables and program constants (resulting from static final declarations in programs; TODO: it is weird that constants are a special case of variables).
Additionally, as a legacy of the past, the RecodeR front end of KeY still creates objects of this class also for fields (aka. attributes, member variables), even though theoretically, these are *not* program variables (not any more)! Such fake "program variables" can be recognized by the fact that calling isMember() on them returns true. Thus, they must never occur in actual proofs. The method HeapLDT.getFieldSymbolForPV() serves to convert such fake program variables to the appropriate constant symbols.

    • getStartPosition

      public Position getStartPosition()
      Description copied from interface: SourceElement
      Returns the start position of the primary token of this element. To get the start position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Specified by:
      getStartPosition in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the start position of the primary token.

      Returns:

      public SourceElement.Position getRelativePosition()

      Returns:

    • match

      public MatchConditions match(SourceData source, MatchConditions matchCond)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramElement
      matches the source "text" (@link SourceData#getSource()) against the pattern represented by this object. In case of a successful match the resulting MatchConditions with the found instantiations of the schemavariables. If the match failed, null is returned instead.
      Specified by:
      match in interface ProgramElement
      Parameters:
      source - the SourceData with the program element to match
      matchCond - the MatchConditions found up to this point
      Returns:
      the resulting match conditions or null if the match failed
